mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in > 55% of months when PAYEMS increases, or rank correlation between PAYEMS change and 20-day returns is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates corporate borrowing costs and reduces equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, or the mean return during triggered periods is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in both the unemployment rate and the consumer price index signal stagflationary pressure that erodes corporate margins and compresses valuations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of UNRATE and CPIAUCSL does not negatively correlate with 20-day forward returns, or single-variable models explain the predictive power without the interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market momentum that triggers rapid risk-off repositioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during late-cycle expansions, or the signal's rank IC is below 0.02 during assigned regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in housing starts signals durable demand for credit-sensitive sectors and forward economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of periods during sustained HOUST increases, or rank correlation is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals improving manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in > 55% of months when INDPRO accelerates, or rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales signals stronger-than-expected consumer spending that supports near-term earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered months despite rising RSAFS, or rank IC is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a month-over-month pause or decline in the federal funds rate signals a dovish pivot that triggers a rapid repricing of terminal rate expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances during tightening cycles, or the mean return is not significantly positive.
